title: tasks-mux: responderType on SDK task definitions (defineTask API)
summary: Extend the SDK defineTask task-definition API so tasks can declare routing intent through responderType. Add typed agent and breakpoint routing metadata, helper APIs for external agent/human/auto tasks, validation that agent responderType requires an adapter, and tests/docs that migrate away from the superseded external: true model.
dependencyNote: This depends on foundational tasks-mux routing work being implemented in parallel. The SDK change should remain backward-compatible and should not implement tasks-mux runtime consumption unless a dependency contract has landed and requires a small compile integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a public ResponderType type and typed AgentTaskOptions for TaskDef.agent.
Extend BreakpointTaskOptions with responderType and routing metadata needed by human/tracker breakpoint routing.
Add adapter and fallbackType fields for agent routing hints.
Implement externalAgentTask() so it emits responderType: "agent" with an adapter instead of external: true.
Implement humanTask() so it emits responderType: "human".
Implement autoTask() so it emits responderType: "auto".
Validate that a non-empty adapter is required when agent.responderType is "agent".
Ensure validation applies to positional and object-form defineTask builds.
Preserve legacy/custom TaskDef structural permissiveness for task definitions that do not opt into responderType.
Add targeted tests for helper output, defineTask serialization/preservation, adapter validation, breakpoint routing metadata, and legacy object-form compatibility.
Update docs/examples that still describe external: true as the primary external-agent contract, noting that #635 supersedes #603 and tasks-mux runtime consumption is related follow-up work.
nonGoals: Do not implement full tasks-mux routing consumption for SDK task effects; that belongs to the parallel foundational work tracked by #630/#620.
Do not remove the TaskDef index signature or otherwise make custom process definitions fail unless they opt into invalid responderType metadata.
Do not redesign defineTask beyond the routing-intent API needed for #635.
Do not require adapter for responderType values other than "agent" unless the design docs or landed foundational contract explicitly require it.
Do not keep external: true as the primary new helper contract.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #635, every comment, labels, related issue mentions, and the routing design docs to lock the SDK API contract and scope boundaries.

plannedPhases.name: Foundation dependency check
plannedPhases.purpose: Check parallel tasks-mux routing work and open PRs for field-name or contract conflicts before implementing the SDK layer.

plannedPhases.name: SDK API design
plannedPhases.purpose: Define responderType, agent and breakpoint option shapes, helper outputs, validation location, and compatibility rules.

plannedPhases.name: Regression plan
plannedPhases.purpose: Add failing tests for helper output, defineTask positional/object-form behavior, adapter validation, breakpoint metadata, and legacy compatibility before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement the focused SDK type/helper/validation/doc changes, iterating up to three attempts based on verification and review findings.

plannedPhases.name: Verification gate
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted task tests, SDK build, SDK tests, metadata verification, and diff hygiene checks.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review public API completeness, object-form compatibility, serializer preservation, docs migration, and non-goal compliance.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm every issue deliverable is satisfied and identify any remaining dependency-driven maintainer decision before PR finalization.

breakpoints.when: The foundational tasks-mux work has landed with incompatible routing field names or semantics.
options: Pause for foundational work to land

breakpoints.when: Final acceptance finds a real ambiguity in the responderType union or adapter validation contract.

qualityGates: New targeted tests fail for the expected missing-implementation reason before code changes.
Helper tests prove externalAgentTask emits responderType: "agent" plus adapter and does not rely on external: true.
defineTask tests prove positional and object-form APIs preserve valid routing metadata and reject agent responderType without adapter.
Compatibility tests prove legacy object-form and custom TaskDef shapes still build when responderType is absent.
Docs/examples no longer present external: true as the primary model for this feature.
npm run test:sdk passes or unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with exact evidence.
Code review approves API shape, dependency boundaries, and migration wording.

publishPlan.prTitle: Add responderType to SDK task definitions
